messages.message: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

messages.message:
Error: Cannot access variable before it is declared

`stopListening` is accessed before it is declared, which prevents the earlier access from updating when this value changes over time.

/Users/lhommeduchili/hacking/resonance/src/components/p2p/useListener.ts:39:18
  37 |     audio.muted = true; // MUST be muted so the browser doesn't play raw audio bypassing WebAudio API
  38 |     audioElementRef.current = audio;
> 39 |     return () => stopListening();
     |                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ `stopListening` accessed before it is declared
  40 |   }, []);
  41 |
  42 |   const createUpstreamConnection = (

  471 |

      |   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 473 |     // Tell server we left to close the PoC session
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 474 |     if (socketRef.current && socketRef.current.connected) {
      …
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 511 |     }
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 512 |   };
      | ^^^^^ `stopListening` is declared here

messages.message: Use "@ts-expect-error" instead of "@ts-ignore", as "@ts-ignore" will do nothing if the following line is error-free.

suggestions.desc: Replace "@ts-ignore" with "@ts-expect-error".

messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

/Users/lhommeduchili/hacking/resonance/src/components/p2p/useNetworkTelemetry.ts:94:13
  92 |         } else {
  93 |             // Reset to clean state when ambient or disconnected
> 94 |             setDataTransferRate(1.0);
     |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  95 |             prevStatsRef.current = {
  96 |                 timestamp: 0,
  97 |                 packetsReceived: 0,

Error: Cannot access refs during render

React refs are values that are not needed for rendering. Refs should only be accessed outside of render, such as in event handlers or effects. Accessing a ref value (the `current` property) during render can cause your component not to update as expected (https://react.dev/reference/react/useRef).

/Users/lhommeduchili/hacking/resonance/src/components/p2p/useSpatialAudio.ts:124:10
  122 |   }, [stream]);
  123 |
> 124 |   return {
      |          ^
> 125 |     isInitialized: isInitializedRef.current,
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 126 |     initAudio,
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 127 |     resumeAudio: () => audioContextRef.current?.resume(),
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 128 |   };
      | ^^^^ Cannot access ref value during render
  129 | }

Error: Cannot access refs during render

React refs are values that are not needed for rendering. Refs should only be accessed outside of render, such as in event handlers or effects. Accessing a ref value (the `current` property) during render can cause your component not to update as expected (https://react.dev/reference/react/useRef).

/Users/lhommeduchili/hacking/resonance/src/components/p2p/useSpatialAudio.ts:125:20
  123 |
  124 |   return {
> 125 |     isInitialized: isInitializedRef.current,
      |                    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Cannot access ref value during render
  126 |     initAudio,
  127 |     resumeAudio: () => audioContextRef.current?.resume(),
  128 |   };
